Document:
# Path of Blade
Difficulty: [color=orange]Medium[/color]
The Path of Blade is as the name suggests. You are highly competent at cutting your opponents to ribbons. Pick this path if you want to fight, and you want to be the best at fighting.
## Pros
- Capable of blocking incoming attacks, retaliating with a riposte. - Rapidly deals damage through dual-wielded blades and channeled strikes. - High defense against stuns and knockdowns. - Highly lethal combatant in a direct combat with a single opponent.
## Cons
- Requires a high degree of skill input. - Without blades, the path loses most of its fighting power. - Lacks mobility options. - Lacks environmental protections.
## Knowledge
Knowledge Name Ingredients/Type Description
The Cutting Edge Two Knives Creates a sundered blade.
Grasp of the Blade Passive Ability Your grasp will stun targets that are lying down or facing away from you.
Mark of the Blade Passive Ability Your grasp will now apply the blade mark, creating flying blades that protect you from attacks when active, throw with an empty hand to use then as a ranged attack.
Champion Hook Active Ability Makes your next blade light attack deal extra damage, knockdown and hook victim.
Sacraments of Power Active Ability Grants a spell that gives you total damage immunity and some damage reflect for 5 seconds, takes 1 second to start up. Cannot attack while active.
Furious Steel Active Ability Summons three flying blades to protect you.
Empowered Blades Passive Ability You can attack with two blades at once, you can also use your grasp to empower blades.
Wolves Among Sheep Active Ability Summons giant arena, forcing everyone trapped inside to fight each other in order to escape.
Maelstrom of Silver Three headless corpses You gain a regenerating ring of blades. Your Furious Steel ability cooldown is halved. You deal more damage with your blades and have lifesteal on attacks. Knockdown and stun time is halved, you also become immune to disarms.
## Armor and Passive Ability
Name Effect
T1 Passive You can perform a riposte every 20 seconds while wielding your blade.
Shattered Panoply Immunity to electrical shocks and stun baton knockdown.
T2 Passive Immunity to having your limbs dismembered, as well as wound resistance below 50% hp.
T3 Passive Riposte cooldown lowered to 10 seconds.
## Tips
- Your Mansus Grasp will stun your opponent if they are attacked from behind or while they are prone. Triggering the mark will grant you a orbiting knife that will protect you from one melee or ranged attack. - You are highly reliant on approaching opponents in melee. Slips and bolas are your worst enemy. You can counteract slips by crafting a pair of Greaves Of The Prophet, or remove restraints Realignment. - Sacraments of Power makes you immune to damage, harming people trying to attack you but you cannot use melee or ranged weapons while it's active. Use it as an escape or rush tool. - With Empowered Blades, your offensive power grows considerably. You are able to fight with dual-wielded blades, and can empower them by activating your Mansus Grasp while wielding your blades. Left click attack with empowered blade on living target triggers grasp effect and applies blade mark. Your blades also deal additional damage to structures. - Maintaining a good offense also creates a good defense. With orbiting blades, you are able to block additional incoming attacks. Remember that you can use throw key with empty hand to throw the orbiting blades, dealing heavy damage and destroying structures. - Use Wolves Among Sheep with caution. Not only does it have a significant cooldown, but it also arms anyone trapped in the effect with you with blades of their own. Use it either as a last ditch defense, or when you know you have the upper hand and need an extra edge. Just don't try to flee the area before taking someone out first.
